title: syngonanthus humboldtii var. parvus
intro: Syngonanthus humboldtii var. parvus is a plant species that belongs to the family Eriocaulaceae and is native to the tropical regions of South America. It is also known as 'São Tomé' or 'Pitomba-do-mato', and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Syngonanthus humboldtii var. parvus include its use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and high blood pressure. Syngonanthus humboldtii var. parvus is traditionally prepared as a tea or decoction by infusing the plant's leaves in hot water, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments.
